verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted ligand canonically found in latent complexes in the extracellular matrix and circulation. However, the latent TGF-β complex can be tethered to the cell surface via integrin binding and other surface-anchoring mechanisms, and activated TGF-β can remain surface-associated during cell-cell signaling. The protein also exhibits dual localization — intracellular pools in secretory vesicles and extracellular matrix deposition alongside surface-tethered latent complexes. This qualifies as contextual surface accessibility via stable surface attachment and dual localization rather than constitutive baseline surface residence.
